codeReview.summary: The feature adds a dedicated native perft regression suite and wires it into the engine test runner. It covers all six standard perft positions, curated legal-move-count checks, divide-perft diagnostics, and the required starting-position depth-5 timing guard. I found no blocking defects; the only review note is that the duplicate-move assertion is narrower than the requirement language because it compares full encoded moves rather than normalized move identity.

issues.description: `move_list_has_duplicates()` compares full `Move` values, but `Move` also encodes flags and captured-piece metadata (`engine/src/movegen.h:30-63`). That means the suite can miss semantically duplicate legal moves if the same source/target/promotion is emitted twice with different metadata bits; combined with the depth-2 recursion limit at `engine/tests/test_perft.c:267`, this does not fully prove the stated 'no duplicate moves generated' requirement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `engine-worker` skill is out of sync with the repo's actual engine layout and commands. It still instructs workers to use `tests/` and `engine/*.c`, while the mission documents and repository tooling use `engine/tests/` and `engine/src/*.c`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/engine-worker/SKILL.md:35-36,42,51-52,63` refer to `tests/` and `engine/*.c`; `AGENTS.md:39,45-46,58` and `.factory/services.yaml:6,11` use `engine/tests/*.c` and `engine/src/*.c`. The reviewed worker transcript skeleton also shows the worker reading `engine/tests/test_main.c` and compiling `engine/src/*.c engine/tests/*.c` instead of following the stale skill paths.

summary: Review result: pass. The feature delivers the expected perft regression coverage and integrates it cleanly into the native engine test runner, with one non-blocking note about duplicate-move verification being narrower than the requirement wording.
